message:
refactor(core): optimize Map update pattern to reduce redundant lookups

Problem:
The existing Map update pattern performs two hash lookups when adding
to a Map bucket:

  if (!map.has(key)) {     // Lookup 1: check existence
    map.set(key, []);      // (set if missing)
  }
  map.get(key)!.push(item); // Lookup 2: retrieve bucket

This is a common anti-pattern that doubles the hash computation cost
for every insert operation.

Solution:
Use a single get() call and branch on the result:

  const bucket = map.get(key);  // Single lookup
  if (bucket) bucket.push(item);
  else map.set(key, [item]);

This reduces hash lookups from 2 to 1 per insert, and eliminates the
non-null assertion (!) which was a code smell indicating the pattern
could be simplified.

Changes:
- src/outcome.ts: applyOutcomeFeedback() updates Map refactored
  - Also adds 'as const' to "other" literal for better type inference
  - Extracts updateItem into named variable for readability
- src/trauma.ts: saveTraumas() batches Map refactored
  - Extracts JSON.stringify(entry) into named 'line' variable

Impact:
- Micro-optimization: ~50% reduction in Map operations per insert
- Code clarity: Removes non-null assertions (!)
- Type safety: Better literal type inference with 'as const'
- No behavioral changes; purely structural refactor
